annotations.label: Nyquist–Shannon sampling theorem

anchor.snippet: The Nyquist–Shannon sampling theorem is a theorem in the field of signal processing

annotations.note: Grep for 'Nyquist' and 'Shannon' in Mathlib returns no signal-processing results; the sampling theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nyquist frequency

anchor.snippet: An important consequence of the sampling theorem is the concept of Nyquist frequency

annotations.note: No occurrence of 'Nyquist' in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sampling

anchor.snippet: Sampling is a process of converting a signal

annotations.note: Signal-processing 'sampling' (discretizing a continuous signal) is not formalized; existing 'sampling' references in Mathlib are probabilistic.

annotations.label: Nyquist rate

anchor.snippet: is called the Nyquist rate and is an attribute of the continuous-time input

annotations.note: No Nyquist-related declarations exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nyquist frequency (sampling equipment)

anchor.snippet: is called the Nyquist frequency and is an attribute of the sampling equipment

annotations.note: No Nyquist-related declarations exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nyquist criterion (Raabe condition)

anchor.snippet: The condition described by these inequalities is called the Nyquist criterion

annotations.note: No Nyquist-related declarations exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sample period / sampling interval

anchor.snippet: is customarily used to represent the interval between adjacent samples and is called the sample period or sampling interval

annotations.note: No formalization of signal sample periods in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sinc interpolation

anchor.snippet: A mathematically ideal way to interpolate the sequence involves the use of sinc functions

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Real.sinc` (sin x / x with value 1 at 0) but no sinc-interpolation reconstruction formula is built on it.

annotations.label: Shannon sampling theorem
annotations.note: Despite `Real.sinc` and Poisson summation existing, the Shannon sampling/reconstruction theorem itself is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Periodic summation / DTFT of samples

anchor.snippet: Then the samples

annotations.note: The DTFT and the periodic summation of a Fourier transform from samples are not formalized; Mathlib only has the abstract Poisson summation identity.

annotations.label: Alias

anchor.snippet: Any frequency component above

annotations.note: Signal-processing aliasing is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Anti-aliasing filter

anchor.snippet: The type of filter required is a lowpass filter , and in this application it is called an anti-aliasing filter

annotations.note: No lowpass / anti-aliasing filter concepts in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Recovery of S(f) from periodic summation

anchor.section: Derivation as a special case of Poisson summation
anchor.snippet: When there is no overlap of the copies

annotations.note: Mathlib has Poisson summation (`Real.tsum_eq_tsum_fourier`) which is the engine, but the bandlimited-recovery statement is absent.

annotations.label: Whittaker–Shannon interpolation formula

anchor.section: Derivation as a special case of Poisson summation
anchor.snippet: The inverse transform of both sides produces the Whittaker–Shannon interpolation formula

annotations.note: Grep for 'Whittaker' returns no hits; the interpolation formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Oversampling

anchor.section: Derivation as a special case of Poisson summation
anchor.snippet: called oversampling , have no effect on the outcome of the reconstruction

annotations.note: Oversampling is a signal-processing notion absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shannon's original derivation

anchor.snippet: Poisson shows that the Fourier series in Eq.1 produces the periodic summation

annotations.note: Shannon's derivation of the sampling theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multivariable signals and images

anchor.section: Application to multivariable signals and images
anchor.snippet: Grayscale images, for example, are often represented as two-dimensional arrays

annotations.note: Image/multivariable sampling examples are not modeled in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Critical-frequency ambiguity examples are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bandpass sampling (non-baseband)

annotations.note: Bandpass sampling is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Engineering example, not in scope of Mathlib.

annotations.note: Bandpass interpolation filter not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shannon nonuniform sampling theorem

anchor.snippet: The Shannon sampling theory for non-uniform sampling states that a band-limited signal can be perfectly reconstructed

annotations.note: Nonuniform sampling theorems are not in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Landau's average-rate bound is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compressed sensing sub-Nyquist reconstruction

anchor.section: Sampling below the Nyquist rate under additional restrictions
anchor.snippet: A non-trivial example of exploiting extra assumptions about the signal is given by the recent field of compressed sensing

annotations.note: Compressed sensing recovery results are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: Another example where sub-Nyquist sampling is optimal
